symbols.name: y_i
symbols.def: i-th ordered outcome value, with y_1<\cdots<y_J

symbols.name: \rho
symbols.def: fixed strictly increasing score with \rho(y_1)=-1 and \rho(y_J)=1

symbols.name: H_\epsilon
symbols.def: matrix with m-th row equal to the negative coefficient row of S_m(r)

symbols.name: b
symbols.def: upper gain-band endpoint with a\le b

symbols.name: E
symbols.def: lexicographically first maximal linearly independent row subsystem of the mass equations and A_mr=b_m equations

symbols.name: e(\eta)
symbols.def: right-hand side corresponding to E, with factual entries b_{md\ell h} and fixed mass entries \lambda_k

symbols.name: \psi_s(\eta)
symbols.role: endpoint as a function of LP inputs

symbols.name: \nu_s^*
symbols.def: equality multiplier at a population saddle point

symbols.name: \gamma_s^*
symbols.def: nonnegative inequality multiplier at a population saddle point

symbols.name: \widehat\eta^{(-f)}
symbols.def: training-fold estimate of \eta_0 excluding fold f

symbols.name: \widehat\psi_s
symbols.def: cross-fitted one-step endpoint estimator in def:one-step-endpoint-estimator

novel.justification: This deliberately discrete cutpoint-saturated design is implemented by an encouragement experiment that randomizes, within each covariate stratum, every uptake probability in a prespecified partition, including zero and one.

This finite-grid shape restriction says that normalized ranks of either potential outcome weakly sort with resistance. It is plausible when resistance indexes an ordered latent trait; at K=1 it is vacuous, preserving the binary audit.

This is an elicitable linear finite-grid sensitivity restriction: a lower bound on conditional concordance of fixed normalized outcome ranks. It represents a common latent trait and, unlike an odds-ratio restriction, is polyhedral in the response-type table.
A reported concordance sensitivity level must be compatible with the finite observed experiment; compatibility is auditable by the displayed LP.
Dual uniqueness is the regular finite-LP stratum, outside basis-change hyperplanes, and can be audited from a primal-dual certificate.
A stable active face holds locally away from basis changes and can be checked by the distance of the fitted solution from inactive faces.
Positive slack separates the reported LP solution from inactive faces in a finite design.
The smallest zero-versus-one encouragement design reveals both potential-outcome margins and isolates common-coupling uncertainty without approximation.
Balanced binary potential-outcome margins make dependence the sole remaining uncertainty in the witness.
The zero-gain band is the policy-relevant probability that treatment leaves a coarsened outcome unchanged.
A positive rank-concordance floor is the transparent binary restriction generated by a common latent trait.

definitions.name: structural identified set
definitions.construction: \operatorname{Id}^{\epsilon}_P(\psi)=\{\psi_\omega(r):r\text{ is induced by one generalized-Roy SCM satisfying ass:threshold-selection, ass:uniform-resistance, ass:iv-exogeneity, ass:rank-sorted-response-types, ass:rank-score-concordance and inducing }P\}

definitions.name: identified-law domain
definitions.construction: \mathcal D_\epsilon=\{P\in\mathcal P(\mathcal Y\times\{0,1\}\times\mathcal Z\times\mathcal X):\alpha_m\pi_{\ell m}>0 ∀(m,\ell),\ \mathcal F_\epsilon(P)\ne\varnothing\}

statements.statement: Under ass:finite-discrete-design, ass:threshold-selection, ass:uniform-resistance, ass:iv-exogeneity, and ass:exact-cutpoint-coverage, every admissible SCM satisfies A_mr=b_m. Conversely, every r satisfying the mass equations and A_mr=b_m is induced by one common law: conditional on X=x_m, draw (I_k,Y(0),Y(1)) with masses r_{mkij}, draw U uniformly on the selected I_k, draw Z independently with probabilities \pi_{\ell m}, and set D=1\{U\le p_{m\ell}\}. In the audit M=K=1,J=L=2,(p_{11},p_{12})=(0,1), the p=1 arm reveals \sum_i r_{11ih}=P(Y(1)=y_h\mid X=x_1), the p=0 arm reveals \sum_j r_{11hj}=P(Y(0)=y_h\mid X=x_1), and this one table supplies the common-law pasting.
statements.justification: Exact endpoint coverage makes the factual sums exhaustive; no partially treated resistance cell is omitted.
statements.gap: The failed response-type path identified a pasting problem but did not supply this endpoint-only factual map and common-law audit.
statements.consumer: It certifies that a feasible constrained-LP table represents one generalized-Roy law before a gain-band interval is reported.

statements.statement: For every P\in\mathcal D_\epsilon, does \operatorname{Id}^{\epsilon}_P(\psi)=[\psi_L(P),\psi_U(P)] with a common generalized-Roy SCM attaining each endpoint? The handle is def:feasible-common-coupling together with thm:factual-map-pasting. Establish also that, for every P\in\mathcal W_\kappa, \operatorname{Id}^{\kappa}_P(\psi)=[(1+\kappa)/2,1]. In the witness t=P(Y(0)=Y(1)\mid X=x_1), S_1(r)=2t-1, and the unrestricted balanced-marginal Frechet set is [0,1]; therefore the common-coupling band set is strictly non-representable by separable/product-cost or assembled marginal/pointwise bounds.
statements.justification: The real open step is sharpness and endpoint attainment under one rank- and copula-restricted response-type law, not numerical optimization of an outer table.
statements.gap: Tan--Blanchet--Syrgkanis require product costs; Firpo--Ridder show why pointwise extrema need not assemble into one distribution, and no cited result supplies this Roy-response-type concordance problem.
statements.consumer: It delivers sharp sensitivity intervals for the policy-weighted share whose individual gain lies in a prespecified band.

statements.statement: Conditional on an affirmative resolution of oeq:sharp-common-coupling, under ass:iid-sampling, ass:positive-observed-cells, ass:unique-dual, ass:active-face-stability, ass:inactive-slack, and ass:score-rate, \dot\psi_s=\partial_\eta L_s(r_s^*,\lambda_s^*;\eta_0), E_P[\phi_s(W)]=0, and the one-step score in def:one-step-endpoint-estimator is Neyman-orthogonal: its expectation has derivative zero at \eta_0 because E_P[\varphi_\eta(W;\eta)] has derivative -I at \eta_0. Hence \sqrt n(\widehat\psi_s-\psi_s(P))\rightsquigarrow\mathcal N(0,\operatorname{Var}_P[\phi_s(W)]) for s\in\{L,U\}. If the optimal dual set is non-singleton, derive the LP-value Hadamard directional derivative and prove validity of a derivative-estimated Fang--Santos bootstrap.
statements.justification: The estimator averages one orthogonal score, rather than adding an LP derivative to an already perturbed empirical LP value.
statements.gap: The generalized-Roy DML template is separable, Ober-Reynolds lacks generated Roy response-type constraints, and Fang--Santos supplies the nonregular resampling principle rather than this score.
statements.consumer: It supplies regular confidence intervals and tie-robust directional inference for sharp gain-band endpoints.

statements.statement: If c_{ij}=u(y_i)+v_0(y_j) and every non-factual constraint in def:feasible-common-coupling factors only through the marginal arrays (\sum_jr_{mkij})_{m,k,i} and (\sum_ir_{mkij})_{m,k,j}, then the endpoint LP separates into the corresponding marginal linear programs. This is the product-cost sanity reduction.
statements.justification: The hypothesis covers rank, sensitivity, and every other non-factual constraint, so it actually implies separability.
statements.gap: This recovers the known product-cost boundary instead of claiming an advance on the anchor's separable estimand.
statements.consumer: It identifies when an empirical implementation may use existing separable PRTE machinery.

sampling_model.design: i.i.d. draws W_i=(Y_i,D_i,Z_i,X_i) from P; p_{m\ell}, q_k, \rho, and \epsilon are fixed design or analyst inputs
sampling_model.units: n independent units with finite X, Z, and Y supports
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: Cross-fitted estimators of the finite cell-probability vector \eta_0 satisfy the score-specific second-order rate; cutpoint incidence is fixed design information.

bibliography.citation: Jiyuan Tan, Jose Blanchet, and Vasilis Syrgkanis (2026), 'Partial Identification of Policy-Relevant Treatment Effects with Instrumental Variables via Optimal Transport,' arXiv:2604.12263.
Philip Marx (2024), 'Sharp Bounds in the Latent Index Selection Model,' Journal of Econometrics, 105561. doi:10.1016/j.jeconom.2023.105561.
Sukjin Han and Shenshen Yang (2024), 'A Computational Approach to Identification of Treatment Effects for Policy Evaluation,' Journal of Econometrics 240(1), 105680.

Yanqin Fan, Emmanuel Guerre, and Dongming Zhu (2017), 'Partial Identification of the Distribution of Treatment Effects and Its Functionals,' Journal of Econometrics. doi:10.1016/j.jeconom.2016.10.005.
Sergio Firpo and Geert Ridder (2019), 'Partial Identification of the Treatment Effect Distribution and Its Functionals,' Journal of Econometrics. doi:10.1016/j.jeconom.2019.04.012.

Brigham R. Frandsen and Lars J. Lefgren (2021), 'Partial Identification of the Distribution of Treatment Effects with an Application to the KIPP Middle Schools,' Quantitative Economics. doi:10.3982/QE1273.
Zijun Gao, Shu Ge, and Jian Qian (2025), 'Bridging Multiple Worlds: Multi-marginal Optimal Transport for Causal Partial-identification Problem,' AISTATS, PMLR 258:721-729.
Daniel Ober-Reynolds (2023), 'Estimating Functionals of the Joint Distribution of Potential Outcomes with Optimal Transport,' arXiv:2311.09435.
Zheng Fang and Andres Santos (2019), 'Inference on Directionally Differentiable Functions,' Review of Economic Studies 86(1):377-412. doi:10.1093/restud/rdy049.
Victor Chernozhukov et al. (2018), 'Double/Debiased Machine Learning for Treatment and Structural Parameters,' Econometrics Journal 21:C1-C68.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Generalized-Roy PRTE CCOT has closed-form bounds and discrete-IV DML after a product-cost separable reduction.
Sharp latent-index bounds target linear MTE functionals using identified marginals and stochastic orders.
Policy evaluation bounds concern weighted-MTE averages under IV restrictions.
Joint potential-outcome functionals are partially identified after conditional marginal recovery.
Pointwise-sharp treatment-effect distribution bounds need not assemble into a uniformly sharp functional bound.
Sharp distributional treatment-effect bounds optimize over one structured admissible copula rather than assemble pointwise extrema.
Causal partial identification with known potential-outcome marginals is represented as multi-marginal optimal transport.
Sharp joint-potential-outcome OT bounds admit dual-based root-n estimation under regularity conditions.
Derivative-estimated resampling is valid for directionally differentiable maps when the ordinary bootstrap fails.

tldr: The proposal studies sharp common-coupling identification and regular/nonregular inference for a nonseparable two-sided gain-band functional in a discrete-IV generalized-Roy model. Exact cutpoint coverage repairs the factual response-type map and yields a common-law pasting lemma. The central open question is whether the rank-sorted, rank-concordance LP exactly equals the structural identified set with endpoint attainment. A binary concordance witness proves strict non-representability by separable or assembled marginal bounds. Conditional on that identification result, a cross-fitted one-step dual score provides a regular root-n target and a Fang--Santos target at dual ties.

project_justification.gap: Product-cost generalized-Roy CCOT does not identify a nonseparable gain-band event, while marginal or pointwise extrema need not arise from one potential-outcome law.
project_justification.niche: A deliberately cutpoint-saturated discrete encouragement design supports policy-weighted shares of individual gains in a specified interval under an interpretable rank-concordance sensitivity analysis.
project_justification.fill: The project supplies the corrected response-type construction, seeks a sharp common-law LP characterization with attainment, and develops a properly cancelling one-step dual-score expansion with directional inference at LP kinks.

related_work: Tan, Blanchet, and Syrgkanis (2026) are the direct generalized-Roy PRTE anchor, but their analytic and DML results use a product-cost reduction absent from the gain-band indicator. Marx (2024) and Han and Yang (2024) study linear or weighted-MTE policy functionals rather than an event of the joint gain law. Fan, Guerre, and Zhu (2017) motivate joint-potential-outcome functionals after marginal recovery. Firpo and Ridder (2019) and Frandsen and Lefgren (2021) establish the pointwise-versus-common-coupling issue. Gao, Ge, and Qian (2025) provide causal multi-marginal OT without this generalized-Roy factual map. Ober-Reynolds (2023) motivates dual OT inference but has no generated Roy response-type constraints. Fang and Santos (2019) supply the directional-resampling framework.
technical_internal_limitation: Exact cutpoint coverage is a deliberately saturated discrete-design subclass. Without it, the displayed endpoint-only factual map omits partially treated resistance cells and is not asserted to be sharp.
honest_scope: The factual-map-and-pasting lemma includes a full p=(0,1) arithmetic audit, and the additive-cost proposition is a sanity reduction. Equality between the structural identified set and the constrained LP, endpoint attainment, the one-step expansion with generated Roy constraints, and directional-bootstrap validity at dual ties remain to be proved. The rank-concordance restriction is a specific linear sensitivity model, not a claim about nonlinear odds-ratio constraints.

novelty_justification.repo_axis: Failed pid_ot_dte_marginal/v1 isolated the pasting obstruction, while failed pid_poc_tau_band/concordance_sharp_dr left interval-band attainment and score control unresolved. This construction makes the exact factual map, common-law pasting, concrete rank-concordance sensitivity, and correcting one-step score explicit.
novelty_justification.published_axis: The contribution is a common-coupling sharpness question for a non-product gain-band cost under finite generalized-Roy rank sorting and concordance. The class \mathcal W_\kappa exhibits a strict non-representability result. Existing generalized-Roy, MTE, generic MOT, and OT-inference papers inform components but do not jointly deliver the response-type map, sharp nonseparable endpoint, and regular/nonregular inference characterization.

literature_checklist.author: Tan, Blanchet, and Syrgkanis
literature_checklist.one_line: Generalized-Roy PRTE OT becomes tractable after a product-cost reduction.

literature_checklist.author: Fan, Guerre, and Zhu
literature_checklist.one_line: Studies partial identification of joint potential-outcome functionals.

literature_checklist.author: Firpo and Ridder
literature_checklist.one_line: Shows the pointwise-versus-uniform-sharpness problem for treatment-effect distribution functionals.

literature_checklist.author: Frandsen and Lefgren
literature_checklist.one_line: Uses a single structured copula to preserve joint feasibility.

literature_checklist.author: Gao, Ge, and Qian
literature_checklist.one_line: Represents causal partial identification with multi-marginal OT.

literature_checklist.author: Ober-Reynolds
literature_checklist.one_line: Develops dual-based inference for joint-potential-outcome OT functionals.

literature_checklist.author: Fang and Santos
literature_checklist.one_line: Provides inference for directionally differentiable optimized functionals.

literature_checklist.author: Han and Yang
literature_checklist.one_line: Computes sharp policy bounds for weighted-MTE targets.
